title: linaria supina subsp. depauperata
intro: Linaria supina subsp. depauperata, also known as the slender toadflax or poor deer's-finger, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria supina subsp. depauperata include its potential to alleviate coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic to treat urinary issues. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
